ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-06-2012 Off Calendar

Off Calendar Jul-20-2012 Set For Order To Show Cause On Sep-18-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-21-2012 Continued To Aug-01-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Order To Show Cause Re: Sanctions As To Albert G. Stoll, Esq. And Edgar W. Hawkyard, Esq.
Off Calendar Feb-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Order to Show Cause re: Sanctions as to Albert G. Stoll, Esq. & Edward W. Hawkyard, Esq.
Dept. 220, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Not Reported.

Both Messrs. Stoll & Hawkyard were present.
9:19 a.m. - The Court takes the bench. The initial Mandatory Settlement in this matter was scheduled for Friday, January 6, 2012. Due to an ongoing jury trial, the Court was unable at hear the matter that time. The Clerk in Department 220 informed all counsel that the MSC was rescheduled to Friday, January 13, 2012 @ 10:00 a.m. On January 13th, neither trial counsel was not present, and the attorneys that were present had unilaterally decided that the MSC could not go forward. The Court informed counsel that this unilateral decision violated the Rules of Court applicable to Mandatory Settlement Conferences. Trial counsel, parties and any claims adjuster needed to settle this matter were to report to Department 220 on Monday, January 23, 2012. On January 23, 2012, plaintiff agreed to resolve this matter through binding arbitration. Defendant agreed to dismiss the cross-complaint, with a waiver of fees and costs. The written agreement to resolve this matter through binding arbitration was signed by the parties & counsel and submitted to the Court.

With regards to today's hearing, the Court informs Messrs. Stoll & Hawkyard that their actions have wasted the Court's time, which is especially egregious at this time due to Courtroom closures as a result of the recent budget crises.

Both Messrs. Stoll & Hawkyard apoligize to the Court for their behavior and the matter was submitted.

The Court issues SANCTIONS in the amount of $250.00 $150.00 to Albert Stoll & Edward Hawkyard, respectfully, for willful to participate in the Mandatory Settlement Conference. These amounts are payable within ten days of today's date. The sanctions are issued personnaly to both Albert Stoll and Edward Hawkyard.

Ntc Of Mtn Opposing Jose Rigoberto Sanchez'S Application For Good Faith Determination
Off Calendar Feb-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling -
DEFENDANT/CROSS DEFENDANT JOSE RIGOBERTO SANCHEZ Application For Good Faith Determination - The application for good faith determination is denied. Even if the evidence submitted with Mr. Sanchez's reply papers is considered, he has not presented any evidence regarding Plaintiff's approximate recovery or his own proportionate liability. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

Off Calendar Jan-23-2012 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day for Mandatory Settlement Conference, having been continued from January 13, 2012.
Judge: James J. McBride; Clerk: Kevin Dougherty; CSR: Not Reported.

Albert G. Stoll, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Tracy Boyd.
Edgar Hawkyard, Esq. appeared for defendant Radu Rugina Bueza, who was not present at today's hearing.
Kevin Hermonson, Esq. appeared for cross-defendant Jose Sanchez, whose appearance was waived for today's hearing.

In accordance with the terms of the agreement, plaintiff agrees to resolve this matter through binding arbitration. Counsel shall submit an executed arbitration agreement, which specifically sets forth the
